# Plan - 2026-06-19

Source: `.github/research/2026-06-19-research.md`

The 06-19 brief surfaced three direct, single-file fixes -- all on the
MCP path -- plus two operational recommendations against the
`auto-implement` backlog. This plan captures all of them and ladders
the engineering work in the order the brief proposes (cheapest +
lowest-blast-radius first, schema-lowering last in its own PR).

## Issues created

| # | Issue | Title | Category | Size |
|---|-------|-------|----------|------|
| 1 | #803 | [mcp] Default empty `properties: {}` on object MCP tool schemas | mcp | small (~1h) |
| 2 | #804 | [mcp] Register `onprogress` + `resetTimeoutOnProgress` on MCP `callTool` | mcp | small (~2h) |
| 3 | #805 | [provider] Codex-style `sanitizeOpenAISchema` for MCP tool schemas | provider | small/medium (~4h) |

All three carry the mandatory `auto-implement,enhancement` label pair
and were verified post-creation with
`gh issue view <n> --json labels`.

## Implementation tasks (engineering breakdown)

These mirror the `How` section of each issue and are reproduced here
so engineering can pick the order without re-reading the brief.

### Task A -- #803 (`src/mcp/client.ts`)

1. Open `src/mcp/client.ts:124-150` (`mapToolInfo`).
2. After the existing missing-schema fallback, add a normalization
   conditional that defaults `inputSchema.properties` to `{}` when
   `inputSchema.type === 'object'` (or array containing `'object'`)
   and `properties` is undefined.
3. Add a regression test with fixture `inputSchema: { type: 'object' }`.
4. Verify `src/mcp/server.ts:71` if it serves tools to external
   clients; apply the same guard there.
5. Run the gate sequence (lint -> typecheck -> format:check ->
   test:coverage -> build).

### Task B -- #804 (`src/mcp/client.ts`)

1. Edit `src/mcp/client.ts:462-466` to extend the `callTool` options
   with `resetTimeoutOnProgress: true` and an empty `onprogress: () =>
   {}`. Keep the existing `AbortController` wall-clock backstop.
2. Add a test mocking `connection.client.callTool` and asserting both
   options are forwarded.
3. Optionally route the empty handler through `logger.info` once #756
   structured logging lands -- not required for this PR.
4. Run the gate sequence.

### Task C -- #805 (`src/providers/transform.ts` + call site)

1. Implement `sanitizeOpenAISchema(schema)` in
   `src/providers/transform.ts` mirroring opencode's lowering: drop
   unsupported `type` values; fold `const` -> single-element `enum`;
   recurse into `properties` / `items` / `prefixItems` / `$defs` /
   `definitions` / `anyOf` / `oneOf` / `allOf`; preserve `$ref` and
   `description`; filter `required` to strings; infer object/array
   types from structural keys; drop unknown JSON Schema keywords.
2. Add `isOpenAIShapedModel(model, providerMeta?)` returning true for
   model ids matching `/^(gpt-|o[13]-)/i` or `providerMeta.sdk ===
   '@ai-sdk/openai'`.
3. At the call site that ships MCP `inputSchema` into the provider
   tool registration (likely `src/providers/sapAiCore.ts` -- search
   for the `tools:` payload assembly), gate the lowering on
   `isOpenAIShapedModel`. Do NOT mutate cached `McpToolInfo`.
4. Port the opencode `transform.test.ts` fixture set (boolean schemas,
   `$ref` chains, `prefixItems`, `const`->`enum`, inferred object /
   array, filtered `required`, unknown keyword drop, preserved
   `description`).
5. Add a negative test asserting non-OpenAI deployments leave the
   schema untouched.
6. Ship as its own PR. Run the gate sequence; CI 40% line coverage
   should be easy to clear with the ported fixtures.

### Suggested merge order

1. **#803** (smallest, highest leverage -- unblocks zero-arg MCP tools
   instantly).
2. **#804** (small, isolates progress / timeout interaction before
   the larger schema PR).
3. **#805** (largest, gets independent review).

## Backlog hygiene actions taken in this run

The brief's Recommendation #2 explicitly called out the zero-`auto-
implement`-label state of the open queue. Done in this run before
filing new issues:

| Action | Issue | Result |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Add `auto-implement` label | #772 (`[config] Demote claude-code and aider`) | labelled |
| Add `auto-implement` label | #775 (`[mcp] Declare roots capability + ListRoots`) | labelled |
| Add `auto-implement` label | #776 (`[mcp] Optional cwd on stdio MCP servers`) | labelled |
| Close as duplicate of merged #801 | #768 (`[tools] Head+tail truncation`) | closed |

`#777` and `#778` were already closed -- no action. `#766` is the same
underlying request as #776 (stdio cwd) -- left for a future grooming
pass to dedupe rather than opening a new ticket today.

After this run `gh issue list --label auto-implement --state open`
returns 6 issues:

- #803 (new, mcp)
- #804 (new, mcp)
- #805 (new, provider)
- #775 (mcp, backfilled)
- #776 (mcp, backfilled)
- #772 (config, backfilled)

## Recommendations not turned into issues

- **Polling cadence change** (brief Recommendation #3): already
  tracked by the now-labelled #772. No new issue.
- **Reference-repo budget rotation policy** (`5-cycle dryness streak
  -> propose demotion`): already inside the body of #772.

## Out of scope

- Any TUI / Ink work: 06-19 brief flagged opencode #32837 explicitly
  as N/A for our renderer.
- Routing-config / StepFun reasoning gate (cline #11597): N/A, no
  StepFun model in `routing-config.json`.
- Default `program.action(prompt)` fallback (cline #11615): N/A,
  Commander already exits non-zero on unknown commands.

## Constitution & process check

All three new issues respect the constitution: SAP AI Core-First
(none introduce a parallel provider stack), Provider Abstraction
(#805 isolates OpenAI-specific lowering behind `isOpenAIShapedModel`
rather than hard-coding a provider check at the MCP layer), and the
shared scope-enum (`mcp` is not in the commit scope-enum, so PRs
implementing #803 / #804 will use `core` or `tools`; #805 will use
`providers`).
